response.justification: This candidate is a genuinely capable senior full-stack engineer with an admirable testing culture, but he is the wrong shape for this role. The position demands 8 years of C++; his professional C++ amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis. His strongest systems credential — low-latency, millions-of-requests-per-second services at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic — was in DLang and lasted under two years, which does not satisfy the 5-year large-scale infrastructure bar. He has also never held a formal technical-leadership role owning cross-team strategy for a compute platform. A fine engineer; a misfit for this req.
strengths: Real low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic)
Exceptional testing and software-craftsmanship depth, including authoring test frameworks and running TDD/Selenium workshops
Broad polyglot and infrastructure breadth (Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, CI/CD) with sustained open-source ownership
concerns: Falls drastically short of the 8-year C++ minimum — under a year of professional C++ work
No formal technical-leadership role leading cross-team strategy or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers
Large-scale infrastructure experience is under two years and DLang-centric, short of the 5-year requirement
